message:
feat(packs): add RegexSet fast path for O(n) safe pattern matching

Add performance optimization for safe pattern matching using regex::RegexSet,
which allows checking all safe patterns in a single linear-time pass instead
of iterating through each pattern individually.

Changes:
- Add `safe_regex_set: Option<regex::RegexSet>` field to Pack struct
  - Built lazily during pack instantiation in PackEntry::get_pack()
  - Only includes patterns compatible with linear-time regex engine
    (excludes patterns with lookahead/lookbehind)

- Add `safe_regex_set_is_complete: bool` field to Pack struct
  - True when RegexSet covers ALL safe patterns (no backtracking patterns)
  - Enables early-exit optimization: if RegexSet misses and is complete,
    we can skip individual pattern checks entirely

- Add Pack::new() constructor
  - Initializes all lazy fields (keyword_matcher, safe_regex_set,
    safe_regex_set_is_complete) to default None/false values
  - Makes pack initialization cleaner and more consistent

- Update matches_safe() with RegexSet fast path
  - First tries RegexSet if available (O(n) single pass)
  - Falls back to individual pattern iteration only when:
    a) No RegexSet built (compilation failed)
    b) RegexSet is incomplete (backtracking patterns exist)
    c) RegexSet missed but incomplete (need to check remaining patterns)

- Update pack tier tests with additional tier assertions
  - Added platform.github, cdn.cloudflare_workers, loadbalancer.nginx to tier 4
  - Added email.ses, featureflags.launchdarkly, payment.stripe to tier 10

Performance impact: Commands matching safe patterns will benefit from O(n)
matching instead of O(n*m) where m is number of safe patterns. Commands that
don't match any safe pattern with a complete RegexSet will see the largest
improvement by avoiding individual pattern iteration entirely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
